WARNING: These test points are for A2779 STANDARD (820-02841, X2523/MLB_S) ONLY. The C/CTO/Max variant (820-02655) has different component designators. Do NOT use these on the C variant.
description: Decision trees for 20V DCPS scenarios. This is the primary diagnostic voltage for M2 Pro boards.
data_source: Adapted from A2681 diagnostic patterns. No A2779_S-specific repair data yet.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws almost nothing. Charger circuit not loading. CD3217 dead, charger IC not accepting input, or PPBUS_AON open.

steps.action: Verify DCPS is delivering 20V to the board
steps.if_missing: DCPS cable fault, connector fault, or USB-C flex disconnected.

steps.action: Check CD3217 (UF400) for signs of life
steps.measure: Probe PP1V05_UPC0_LDO_CORE (internal LDO output of UF400)
steps.if_missing: UF400 dead. Check for short on supply pins. Try other ports (UF500 page 75, UF600 page 83).
Charger IC not converting. Check CHGR_AUX_OK (page 30), SYS_DETECT (J5150/U5150).
U5700 not starting. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN from J5150. If enable asserted but no output → U5700 dead.
steps.if_present: PP3V8_AON present but 0A draw → PMU not loading. Check U7700 and U8100.

scenarios.range: 0.05–0.5A (low current — partial activity, likely short holding board down)
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing some current but well below normal boot range. Shorted rail clamping regulator or very early boot state.

steps.measure: Sequential probe: PPBUS_AON → PP3V8_AON → PP5V_S2 → MPMU LDOs → SPMU LDOs
steps.if_all_present: All main rails present. Go to step 5.

steps.action: PP3V8_AON missing — investigate U5700 / short on 3V8 line
steps.measure: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON distribution caps (VDDMAIN, WLBT)
steps.if_shorted: PP3V8_AON shorted. Thermal cam. Check VDDMAIN distribution.
steps.if_not_shorted: U5700 enable fault or U5700 dead.

steps.measure: Check P5V2TPS_PWR_EN signal and UC259 components

steps.action: PMU LDO output missing — thermal cam on that rail
steps.measure: Thermal camera focused on the shorted/missing LDO rail

steps.action: All main rails present — check Austringer and CLVR outputs
steps.if_austringer_missing: U9300 fault. Check FIVSDOH_FAULT_L. Check power stage caps (pages 49-50).
steps.if_clvr_missing: CLVR instance fault. Check CLVR address straps (page 54). Note S vs C/D differences.
steps.if_all_present: SoC domain or I2C bus fault. Check SoC reset signals.

scenarios.range: 0.5–1.5A (partial boot — board powering up but stalling)
scenarios.meaning: Board drawing boot-level current but not completing. SoC powered but stalling during firmware load or display init. Could be boot loop.

steps.action: Observe current pattern — stable or cycling?

steps.measure: Check NAND power rails: PPVDS_MAME_NAND_VCC, PP1V2_MAME_NAND_VCCQ
steps.if_missing: NAND power fault. Check SSD power ICs and fuses.
steps.if_present: SSD powered but may be dead or firmware corrupted. Try DFU restore.
steps.note: A2779_S has dual SSD (SSD0 pages 100-104, SSD1 pages 105-109). Check both.

steps.measure: Check display power sequencer (UP710/UP730/UP701 page 114), Luxe backlight (UP800 page 115)
steps.if_backlight_missing: Display power sequencer or Luxe (UP800) fault.

steps.action: Check SoC DVFS domains via CLVR/Austringer
steps.if_all_present: SoC powered, display powered, stalled → DFU restore.

scenarios.meaning: NORMAL operating range for M2 Pro at 20V. 1.5-2.5A typical boot/idle. 2.5-3.5A under load. If customer reports fault and board draws normal current, fault is peripheral not power-related.

steps.action: Board is booting — check for display output
steps.measure: Look for Apple logo, backlight, chime
steps.if_display_working: Board boots. Check peripherals: keyboard, trackpad, speakers, USB-C ports, HDMI (page 89), SD card (page 94), TouchID (pages 129-131).

steps.action: No display but normal current — check display chain

sub_checks.if_no: Display power sequencer (UP710/UP730/UP701) or Luxe (UP800) fault.

sub_checks.if_yes: LCD panel or eDP flex fault. Replace display.

steps.action: Specific peripheral faults at normal current

common_faults.check: Test all 3 ports individually. Specific CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UF600) fault.
HDMI connector (JH500 page 89), Madea retimer (page 91), eSD protection.
SD controller UK150 (GL9755S page 94), SD card slot connector.
Fan connectors JE500/JE510 (page 67), UE510 level shifter.

scenarios.meaning: Excessive current. Either dead-shorted rail or board under heavy GPU/charging load.

steps.action: Is the board functional at this current?
steps.if_functional: May be under heavy load (charging + GPU). Monitor — should settle to 1.5-2.5A.

steps.if_found: Identify component → cross-reference with rails/ICs → replace.

steps.measure: Diode mode on major rails: PPBUS_AON → PP3V8_AON → PP5V_S2 → PP3V3_S2 → Austringer output → CLVR outputs
steps.process: Find which rail reads short. Thermal cam that area.
